task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Data from the CSV file is imported into Vital Recorder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A track is visible in the main view of Vital Recorder, indicating that data has been loaded. The track name or import dialog may reference 'prototype_pleth.csv'.

task_completion.description: The PPG waveform is visible and correctly scaled
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waveform in the track view shows characteristic pulsatile peaks and troughs of a photoplethysmogram (PPG). It is scaled appropriately so that the waveform fits vertically within the track view without being a flat line or clipped.
The title bar of the Vital Recorder window displays 'device_test_import.vital' or 'device_test_import', or a save dialog shows the file being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' with this name.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the waveform is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of the screenshot being saved as 'import_verification.png' to 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\', such as a save dialog from a screenshot tool, the file appearing on the Desktop, or an image viewer displaying the saved file at that path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to import data and save the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Vital Recorder's menus (e.g., File -> Import, File -> Save) rather than using terminal commands or scripts to generate the output files.

integrity.description: The waveform displayed is genuine time-series data within the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The waveform is rendered inside the Vital Recorder track view with appropriate axes and UI elements, not just a static image opened in an image viewer.

integrity.description: The agent captured an actual screenshot of the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the use of a screenshot tool (like Snipping Tool or a built-in OS feature) capturing the Vital Recorder window, rather than downloading or moving a pre-existing image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vital Recorder is a legitimate application used to view physiological data. The expected output project file is 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\device_test_import.vital'. The expected screenshot is 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\import_verification.png'. The PPG (photoplethysmogram) waveform should display characteristic pulsatile peaks and troughs typical of real physiological data recorded at 500 Hz.
